inputs.prompt:
You are a credit analyst completing the documentation section of a financial spread for Alphabet Inc. The data has already been arithmetic-validated. Produce only factual, sourced observations — no qualitative opinions or forward-looking commentary.
Document material items for each period: a) INVESTMENT GAINS: If Gains (Losses) on Investments, Net > $500M
   in any period, note the amount and state it is non-recurring
   (equity mark-to-market). Cite as "(per 10-Q/10-K Other income
   (expense), net footnote)".
b) RESTRUCTURING: If Restructuring and Other Charges is present,
   note the amount and nature.
c) SEGMENT TRENDS: Note any segment where revenue or OI grew or
   declined more than 15% year-over-year. State exact amounts.
d) EPS: Confirm diluted EPS and state shares outstanding. e) TAX RATE: Note if effective tax rate differs from ~21% statutory
   rate by more than 3 percentage points.

Return a JSON object with exactly two keys:
  "segment_names": {
    "[1]": "Google Services",
    "[2]": "Google Cloud",
    "[3]": "Other Bets"
  },
  "notes": [
    {
      "period": "period label e.g. Q1 2025",
      "line_item": "exact template field name",
      "note": "factual one-sentence finding: amount and source reference"
    },
    ...
  ]

Rules for "notes": - 4–8 entries covering material items and any cross-check failures - Every note must state a specific dollar amount or percentage - Every note must end with a source reference: "(per 10-Q Note N)" - No qualitative opinions, no forward-looking statements
